1.title: The Preacher Introduces Himself
1.text: Qoheleth (the 'Preacher' in Hebrew) reveals his position as 'son of David, king in Jerusalem' and begins his reflection on vanity.

1.question: According to the Preacher in chapter 1, what is the state of all things?
options: All things are permanent and lasting
All things are just and equitable

1.question: What does the Preacher say about renewal in the world?
options: All things are new each day
There is always something new under the sun
There is nothing new; all repeats

1.question: What conclusion does the Preacher reach after observing 'all that is done under the sun'?
options: It is all wise and well-made
It is all corruption and sickness
It is vanity and vexation of spirit
It is all comprehensible and rational

1.question: What does the Preacher say about wisdom?
options: In much wisdom is much grief
Only fools suffer; the wise do not

2.title: The Preacher Seeks Pleasures
2.text: He decided to try laughter, joy, wine, and sensory pleasures to see if life has value.

2.question: How did the Preacher build and accumulate possessions?
options: He made pools of water, orchards, and gardens
He received from God without effort

2.question: After accumulating all he desired, what was the Preacher's conclusion?
options: He saw it was great deception
He discovered it was all vanity

2.question: What is the difference between the wise and the fool according to the Preacher?
options: The wise always prosper and the fool suffers
The wise has light in his eyes; the fool walks in darkness
The fool is happier than the wise

2.question: What does the Preacher question about his labors?
options: Who knows if he will be wise or a fool to inherit all

2.question: To whom does God grant wisdom and knowledge according to the Preacher?
options: To a man who is good in His sight
Only to the rich and powerful

3.title: A Time for Everything
3.text: The Preacher presents the idea that everything has its appointed time: birth, death, planting, harvest, war, and peace.

3.question: Complete: 'A time to seek, and a time to ___'
What is the work that God has given to men?
options: To occupy themselves and be afflicted

3.question: What has God put in men's hearts?
What conclusion does the Preacher draw about the best thing for man?
options: To rejoice and enjoy his labor

4.title: Oppression in the World
4.text: The Preacher observes the oppressed without a comforter, seeing that there is vanity in all labor that man does.

4.question: What is better according to the Preacher?
options: A handful with quiet than both hands with toil

4.question: What advantage has a companion according to the Preacher?
options: If one falls, the other will lift him up

4.question: What does the Preacher observe about the new king who ascends to the throne?
options: He is praised by all eternally
There is no end to his popularity
Those who come after will not rejoice in him
He reigns guaranteed for a hundred years

5.title: Warning Against Rash Vows
5.text: The Preacher warns against making rash and easy vows to God; it is better not to vow than to vow and not fulfill.

5.question: What is better according to the Preacher?
What truth does the Preacher affirm about he who loves money?
What happens when wealth is lost?
options: It returns to its owner naturally

5.question: How should the laborer's life be according to the Preacher?
options: To enjoy his labor; this is a gift from God
To accumulate as much as possible

6.title: A Great Evil
6.text: The Preacher observes the man to whom God gave wealth but does not let him enjoy it; another enjoys it in his place.

6.question: According to the Preacher, which life is better?
options: To live a thousand years twice
A stillborn is better than to live long in vanity

6.question: What does the Preacher not know?
options: What is good for man; what will be after him

7.title: In Praise of Wisdom
7.text: The Preacher begins to exalt wisdom, recognizing that no one can be successful without it.

7.question: What is better according to the Preacher?
options: The brief anger of the wise

7.question: What is the benefit of wisdom and money?
options: Both protect, but wisdom preserves the life of its possessors

7.question: What advice does the Preacher give?
options: Be extremely just or extremely ungodly
Be neither too just nor too ungodly

7.question: What truth does the Preacher state about men?
options: There is not a just man who does good and never sins

7.question: What did the Preacher find about men and women?
options: One upright man among a thousand and no upright woman
Women are more righteous than men

8.title: Wisdom Illuminates
8.text: A man's wisdom illuminates his face, changing his severity. The Preacher reflects on obedience to the king.

8.question: According to the Preacher, who will resist the king?
What truth does the Preacher affirm?
options: No one has power over the wind

8.question: What will be the fate of the wicked?
options: His days will be prolonged as a shadow

8.question: What conclusion does the Preacher reach?
options: He understands all of God's work
Though the wise thinks to know, he cannot comprehend God's work
He knows exactly what God does

9.title: Death Comes to All
9.text: Death comes to the just and unjust, the clean and unclean, to the sacrificer and to those who do not sacrifice.

9.question: What does the Preacher affirm about the dead?
options: They still know all that occurs
They know nothing more; their memory is forgotten

9.question: What advice does the Preacher give?
options: Whatever your hand finds to do, do it with all your might

9.question: What illustration does the Preacher use about wisdom?
options: Little wisdom preserves a great city
Rich cities do not need wisdom

9.question: What does the folly of a single fool do?
options: It destroys much wisdom and honor

10.title: Dead Flies and Folly
10.text: Dead flies cause the perfumer's ointment to putrefy. A little folly ruins much wisdom.

10.question: How should one behave when facing someone who is furious?
options: If the spirit of the ruler rises against you, do not abandon your place

10.question: What danger is there in risky activities?
options: He who digs a pit falls into it

10.question: What does the fool constantly do?
options: Multiplies words about things he does not know

10.question: According to the Preacher, what should we not even do?
options: Even in thought curse the king

11.title: Cast Your Bread Upon the Waters
11.text: The Preacher encourages generosity: cast your bread upon the waters and after many days you will find it.

11.question: Why should we be generous according to the Preacher?
options: We will always know when we will prosper
We do not know what misfortune may come
Only the rich should be generous

11.question: What advice does the Preacher give to young people?
options: Rejoice in your youth; light is sweet

12.title: Remember Your Creator
12.text: In youth, remember your Creator. The body ages and death approaches; all is vanity.

12.question: How does the Preacher describe the aging process?
options: The silver cord loosens, the golden bowl breaks

12.question: What is the final conclusion of the book?
options: Vanity is the meaning of life
Fear God and keep His commandments; this is the whole duty of man
Death is the end of all without judgment
